.zsh-entrypoint.content:
# .zsh-entrypoint -- Devcontainer zsh entrypoint.
#
# This file is the controlled entrypoint for zsh in the devcontainer.
# It is loaded via the ZDOTDIR chain:
#
#   devcontainer.json sets ZDOTDIR=/home/ubuntu/m/.devcontainer
#   → .devcontainer/.zshrc sources this file
#   → this file sources the standard zsh config files
#
# NOTE: The ZDOTDIR approach is necessary because zsh has no --init-file
# equivalent. ZDOTDIR redirects where zsh looks for .zshrc, giving us
# a single point of control before the standard configs load.

export HISTSIZE=100000
export SAVEHIST=100000

[[ -f "$HOME/.zshenv" ]] && source "$HOME/.zshenv"
[[ -f "$HOME/.zprofile" ]] && source "$HOME/.zprofile"
[[ -f "$HOME/.zshrc" ]] && source "$HOME/.zshrc"

.zshrc.content:
# ZDOTDIR entrypoint for devcontainer.
#
# REQUIRED: This directory is set as ZDOTDIR in devcontainer.json.
# When zsh starts, it reads $ZDOTDIR/.zshrc instead of ~/.zshrc,
# which lets us intercept startup and route through .zsh-entrypoint.
# Without this, zsh would read ~/.zshrc directly and we'd have no
# devcontainer-specific entrypoint.

source "$HOME/m/.devcontainer/.zsh-entrypoint"

BUILD.bazel.content:
load("//kernel:fmt.bzl", "fmt_test")
load("//kernel:tagged.bzl", "tagged_file")

# All git-tracked files in this package must be registered with Bazel.
exports_files([
    ".zsh-entrypoint",
    ".zshrc",
    "devcontainer.json",
    "docker-compose.macos.yml",
    "docker-compose.yml",
    "init-host.clj",
    "post-attach.clj",
    "post-start.clj",
])

fmt_test(
    name = "build_bazel_fmt",
    src = "BUILD.bazel",
    tool = "buildifier",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "zsh_entrypoint_fmt",
    src = ".zsh-entrypoint",
    tool = "shfmt",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "zshrc_fmt",
    src = ".zshrc",
    tool = "shfmt",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "devcontainer_json_fmt",
    src = "devcontainer.json",
    tool = "biome",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "docker_compose_macos_yml_fmt",
    src = "docker-compose.macos.yml",
    tool = "yq",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "docker_compose_yml_fmt",
    src = "docker-compose.yml",
    tool = "yq",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "init_host_fmt",
    src = "init-host.clj",
    tool = "cljstyle",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "post_attach_fmt",
    src = "post-attach.clj",
    tool = "cljstyle",
)

fmt_test(
    name = "post_start_fmt",
    src = "post-start.clj",
    tool = "cljstyle",
)

# File type tags
tagged_file(
    name = "build_bazel_tag",
    src = "BUILD.bazel",
    tags = [
        "bazel",
        "bazel-build",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "devcontainer_json_tag",
    src = "devcontainer.json",
    tags = [
        "config",
        "devcontainer",
        "json",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "docker_compose_macos_yml_tag",
    src = "docker-compose.macos.yml",
    tags = [
        "config",
        "docker",
        "yaml",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "docker_compose_yml_tag",
    src = "docker-compose.yml",
    tags = [
        "config",
        "docker",
        "yaml",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "init_host_tag",
    src = "init-host.clj",
    tags = [
        "clojure",
        "script",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "post_attach_tag",
    src = "post-attach.clj",
    tags = [
        "clojure",
        "script",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "post_start_tag",
    src = "post-start.clj",
    tags = [
        "clojure",
        "script",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "zsh_entrypoint_tag",
    src = ".zsh-entrypoint",
    tags = [
        "config",
        "devcontainer",
        "shell",
    ],
)

tagged_file(
    name = "zshrc_tag",
    src = ".zshrc",
    tags = [
        "config",
        "devcontainer",
        "shell",
    ],
)

[fmt_test(
    name = "dispatch_cue_fmt",
    src = src,
    tool = "cue",
) for src in glob(["dispatch.cue"], allow_empty = True)]

[tagged_file(
    name = "dispatch_cue_tag",
    src = src,
    tags = [
        "cue",
        "source",
    ],
) for src in glob(["dispatch.cue"], allow_empty = True)]

docker-compose.macos.yml.content:
# Compose override for the macOS dev-bootstrap path. Layered as
# `-f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.macos.yml`. Only the
# `registry` sidecar differs; the Linux flow never reads this file.
#
# Base binds 127.0.0.1:5000 inside `service:dev`'s namespace.
# On Linux that namespace is the host (network_mode: host) and the
# port is reachable from dev-push and from k3d via host.k3d.internal.
# On macOS Docker Desktop "host" is the VM's namespace -- the macOS
# host can't reach VM loopback, so we move the registry to a bridge
# network and publish 127.0.0.1:5000:5000 on the macOS host.
#
# Local-trust assumption (parity with Linux): no auth on the registry,
# only TLS via custom CA. Loopback bind eliminates remote-network
# exposure; any process on the macOS host can still pull/push. Don't
# relax the 127.0.0.1: prefix on the published port without revisiting
# the trust model. See AIDR-00124 Triage.
services:
  registry:
    # !reset null clears the base file's `network_mode: "service:dev"`
    # before `networks:` adds the project-private `dev` network.
    # Without the reset, Compose reports "mutually exclusive
    # network_mode and networks" because scalar inheritance keeps the
    # base value alive. Requires Compose >= 2.24.
    network_mode: !reset null
    # Project-private named network; do NOT use `network_mode: bridge`
    # which puts the container on Docker's shared system bridge where
    # any co-resident Compose stack on the same Docker Desktop could
    # reach it.
    networks:
      - dev
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000"
    environment:
      # Merges with base env list; same-key entries here win.
      # Base bound 127.0.0.1; published port needs 0.0.0.0 inside.
      - REGISTRY_HTTP_ADDR=0.0.0.0:5000
networks:
  dev:

docker-compose.yml.content:
# Docker Compose for defn devcontainer.
#
# All sidecars use network_mode: "service:dev" to share the dev
# container's network namespace (like a k8s pod). Services are
# reachable at localhost from inside the dev container.
#
# All services use init: true (tini) to reap zombie processes.
#
# Data volumes persist across container restarts. Use
# "docker compose down" to stop services (preserves volumes),
# "docker compose down -v" to also delete data.
services:
  dev:
    image: defn.dev/devcontainer/dev:edge
    init: true
    volumes:
      # Bind mount the host repo so dev-sync can git pull and rebuild.
      - ../../:/workspace
      # Docker socket for building images from inside the container.
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Named volumes for state that persists across container rebuilds.
      - home-mise:/home/ubuntu/.local/share/mise
      - home-cache:/home/ubuntu/.cache
      - home-config:/home/ubuntu/.config
      - home-claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude
      - home-dotfiles:/home/ubuntu/.dotfiles
      - code-server-extensions:/home/ubuntu/.local/share/code-server/extensions
      - home-afs:/var/lib/artifact-fs
      - tailscale-state:/var/lib/tailscale
    user: ubuntu
    command: sleep infinity
    # Host network so sidecars share localhost with the dev container.
    network_mode: host
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
      # SYS_ADMIN required for FUSE mounts (artifact-fs).
      - SYS_ADMIN
    # AppArmor on Ubuntu hosts blocks FUSE mounts by default; unconfined
    # lets artifact-fs mount /dev/fuse from inside the container.
    security_opt:
      - apparmor=unconfined
    devices:
      - /dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun
      # FUSE device for artifact-fs mounts. artifact-fs is installed
      # but not started automatically -- run manually when needed.
      - /dev/fuse:/dev/fuse
    environment:
      - DEVCONTAINER=1
      - REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
      - POSTGRES_URL=postgresql://postgres@localhost:5432/postgres
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres@localhost:5432/postgres
      - PATH=/home/ubuntu/.local/share/mise/shims:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin
  redis:
    image: defn.dev/devcontainer/redis
    init: true
    network_mode: "service:dev"
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD
        - redis-cli
        - ping
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128M
  postgres:
    image: defn.dev/devcontainer/postgres
    init: true
    network_mode: "service:dev"
    volumes:
      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD
        - pg_isready
        - -U
        - postgres
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 10s
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512M
  bazel-remote:
    image: defn.dev/devcontainer/bazel-remote
    init: true
    network_mode: "service:dev"
    volumes:
      - bazel-remote-data:/data
    command:
      - --http_address=127.0.0.1:8080
      - --grpc_address=127.0.0.1:9092
      - --profile_address=127.0.0.1:6060
      - --max_size
      - "50"
    healthcheck:
      disable: true
    restart: always
  # Registry: TLS via custom CA, NO auth. Local-trust assumption --
  # any process on the developer's host can pull/push. Loopback bind
  # eliminates remote-network exposure; matching exposure on macOS
  # via the docker-compose.macos.yml override. Adding auth is a
  # multi-platform design problem tracked in AIDR-00124 Triage.
  registry:
    image: defn.dev/devcontainer/registry
    init: true
    network_mode: "service:dev"
    volumes:
      - registry-data:/var/lib/registry
      - registry-tls-certs:/certs:ro
    environment:
      - REGISTRY_STORAGE_DELETE_ENABLED=true
      - REGISTRY_STORAGE_REDIRECT_DISABLE=true
      - REGISTRY_HTTP_ADDR=127.0.0.1:5000
      # The registry serves the LEAF cert (registry-cert.pem) signed
      # by registry-ca.pem. Clients trust registry-ca.pem; serving
      # the CA cert directly would violate Go's strict x509 (CA cert
      # used as TLS leaf is non-compliant). See AIDR-00126.
      - REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_CERTIFICATE=/certs/registry-cert.pem
      - REGISTRY_HTTP_TLS_KEY=/certs/registry-key.pem
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD
        - wget
        - -q
        - --no-check-certificate
        - -O
        - /dev/null
        - https://localhost:5000/v2/
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s
    restart: always
volumes:
  home-mise:
  home-cache:
  home-config:
  home-claude:
  home-dotfiles:
  home-afs:
  code-server-extensions:
  postgres-data:
  bazel-remote-data:
  redis-data:
  registry-data:
  tailscale-state:
  registry-tls-certs:
    external: true

init-host.clj.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bbs
;; Host-side initialization -- runs on the developer's machine
;; before the devcontainer starts. Verifies the edge image is
;; built, builds any missing sidecar images, and ensures the
;; registry-tls-certs docker volume is populated. The same
;; sequence is invoked by `mise run dev-bootstrap` (macOS path).

(require '[defn :refer :all]
         '[devcontainer :as dc])


(dc/check-edge-image!)
(dc/ensure-sidecar-images!)
(dc/ensure-registry-tls-certs-volume!)


(log-ok "host init complete")

post-attach.clj.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bbs
;; Runs on every editor attach (postAttachCommand). The volume +
;; socket chown calls are repeated from post-start.clj because
;; stacks brought up via `mise run dev-bootstrap` (macOS) bypass
;; postStartCommand; the functions are idempotent no-ops when
;; ownership is already correct.

(require '[defn :refer :all]
         '[devcontainer :as dc])


(dc/fix-volume-ownership!)
(dc/chown-docker-socket!)
(dc/ensure-pitchfork-supervisor!)
(dc/start-pitchfork-services!)


(log-ok "post-attach complete")

post-start.clj.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bbs
;; Container-side initialization -- runs once after the container
;; starts. Phases run in dependency order: ownership fixes first
;; (so subsequent file writes succeed), then mise trust, then
;; .bazelrc.user setup, then bootstrap-bazelrc. fix-volume-ownership!
;; and chown-docker-socket! are also called from post-attach.clj
;; to handle stacks brought up via `mise run dev-bootstrap` (macOS),
;; which bypasses postStartCommand entirely; the calls are
;; idempotent no-ops when ownership is already correct.

(require '[defn :refer :all]
         '[devcontainer :as dc])


(dc/fix-volume-ownership!)
(dc/chown-docker-socket!)
(dc/trust-workspace-mise-configs!)
(dc/setup-bazelrc-user!)
(dc/run-bootstrap-bazelrc!)


(log-ok "post-start complete")
